text: Center offices. Mount Millie is now two years into operation and has three full-time employees and ten volunteers. After years of begging, I never got Trista to accept my surrogacy fund to help her find a property, but she did allow me to sell her a part of the mountain so she could build her own rescue center. A large, fully heated Morton building with several divided pasture enclosures resides about a half mile down the road. My brothers and I did all the work on it, and I’m proud to say it’s one of the first rescue centers in Colorado operating fully off sustainable resources. Mount Millie Rescue is open to visitors on the weekends and has a separate drive leading up to it. Business is good, and Trista gets to live her dream of essentially working from home, making the mom-boss vibe her bitch. Her words, not mine. “Stevie!” I call up the stairs to our five-year-old, and seconds later, I hear the loud clomp of her cowboy boots trudging down the steps. My eyes move up her battered knees covered in filthy Band-Aids to her pink sequined skirt and white lace top that’s covered in dirt. She holds a doll in her arms and says, “What, Dad?” I laugh at her bothered tone. “Your mom has something she wants to tell you.” Trista steps out of Millie’s pen and stares up at Stevie standing on the steps. “Mount Millie is getting reindeer!” Stevie’s eyes go wide. “Like Santa’s reindeer?” “Yes! Can you believe it?” “I can believe it,” Stevie says firmly as she continues down the steps. “I asked Santa for them for Christmas. Are we getting penguins too?” Trista laughs as our girl comes to join us down in the barn. “No penguins yet, I’m afraid. Do you want to come help me get their pen set up?” Stevie pushes her hair out of her face and sighs. “Yes, I do, but I want to bring my girls with me, so I’ll be right back.” Trista rolls her eyes as Stevie begins trudging back up the stairs. “You know, you’re not very helpful with your arms full of all your dolls.” “Oh, Mother,” Stevie replies with a growl. Trista’s eyes widen. “Did she just ‘oh, Mother’ growl at me?” “Oh, I think she did,” I reply with a laugh and make my way over to my beautiful wife. Her lips purse gently. “I don’t know how I feel about that.” “You should feel very good,” I reply, cupping my hand on Dax’s back while wrapping my other one around Trista. “She called me a grandpa the other day.” “She did?” Trista bursts out laughing and then sobers, her nose wrinkling at me. “You are kind of old. Look at all these gray hairs coming in.” “These are your fault!” “My fault? What did I do?” “Your existence is very stressful on me.” I smirk and lean in to kiss her. “Oh, I’m so sorry. Whatever can I do to make it up to you?” I shrug and press a kiss to my son’s wild mane of chestnut curls. “I’ll take another baby when you get a chance.” “Hey, man, we have rules, remember? Only one kid in diapers at a time, and this little guy is still in diapers.” “I know, but you make it look so easy.” “Do I?” she exclaims with a laugh. “I kind of wish we could get a surrogate for the next one because this extra twenty pounds I’m carrying on my ass and belly sure don’t feel easy.” “Watch your tone,” I state firmly as my hand gropes her big, beautiful ass. “Those are two of my favorite features.” She rolls her eyes and smirks happily up at me. “Is there anything else I can get you besides another kid?” “That should do for now.” She glares before dragging me down to her lips for a quick peck. I move in closer and cradle her cheek, causing a sweet noise to escape her lips when I deepen the kiss, thanking her for this beautiful life that’s given me these gray hairs.
